data.title: As a DSO user, my explicitly-reported bugs skip the intent search gate
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update fix-bug Gate 1a and debug-everything bug-fix mode to skip intent-search dispatch for bugs tagged CLI_user.
**Why**: The intent search wastes ~20 tool calls and can auto-close user-reported bugs as "working as designed," overriding the user's explicit judgment. Skipping it for CLI_user-tagged bugs respects user authority and saves tokens.
**Scope**:
- IN: fix-bug SKILL.md Step 1.5 tag check before intent-search dispatch, debug-everything SKILL.md bug-fix mode tag extraction from ticket show, negative-path assertion that untagged bugs still dispatch intent-search normally
- OUT: The tagging mechanism itself (story 1), agent guidance for when to tag (story 3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, /dso:fix-bug processing a bug tagged CLI_user records GATE_1A_RESULT="intent-aligned" without dispatching the dso:intent-search sub-agent
  ← Satisfies: "/dso:fix-bug Gate 1a skips the intent-search agent dispatch for bugs tagged CLI_user"
- When this story is complete, /dso:debug-everything processing a CLI_user-tagged bug in bug-fix mode records GATE_1A_RESULT="intent-aligned" without dispatching the intent-search sub-agent
  ← Satisfies: "When /dso:debug-everything processes a CLI_user-tagged bug in bug-fix mode, the inlined fix-bug execution records GATE_1A_RESULT=intent-aligned without dispatching the intent-search sub-agent"
- When this story is complete, bugs without the CLI_user tag still dispatch the intent-search sub-agent normally through Gate 1a
  ← Satisfies: "Gate 1a intent-search dispatches normally for untagged bugs"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Needs both positive test (CLI_user → skip) and negative test (untagged → dispatch)
- [Reliability] debug-everything tag extraction must handle tickets with no tags field gracefully (legacy tickets pre-dating tags support)
